questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: Although the new variety of hybrid seeds developed by the
agricultural institute has show remarkable resistance to pests,
it requires a significantly higher amount of water for irrigation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (2). Use HAS SHOWN in place of HAS SHOW. 📌 Error concept:– The structure of Present Perfect Tense is: Subject + has/have + Past Participle (V3). Here, the clause talks about a completed development whose effect continues in the present - the hybrid seeds now possess resistance. Therefore, Present Perfect Tense is correctly intended, but the verb form is wrong. The verb “show” must appear in its past participle (V3) form after has i.e. V3 → shown ✅

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Her sharp intellect allowed her to ________ the diplomatic pleasantries and address the underlying tension that was palpable in the conference room.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - The correct phrasal verb is "cut through," which means to get past something that blocks your way or to deal with something quickly and directly by ignoring unnecessary details (like "diplomatic pleasantries"). In this context, it implies her intellect allowed her to bypass the small talk and reach the core issue. Meanings and Explanation of Options: Cut off - बीच में रोकना / काट देना - To interrupt someone or to stop a supply/connection. Example: The heavy storm cut off the electricity supply to the entire village for two days. Cut back - कटौती करना - To reduce the amount or consumption of something. Example: If we want to save for a new car, we must cut back on our monthly dining expenses. Cut through - बात की तह तक पहुँचना / रास्ता बनाना - To get past something that blocks your way or to deal with the main part of a problem. Example: Her clear explanation helped us cut through the confusion and understand the new policy. Cut down - कम करना / छोटा करना - To reduce the size, amount, or quantity of something. Example: The doctor advised him to cut down on smoking to improve his lung capacity.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Ancient rituals often required the participants to perform grueling physical tasks in order to atone at their perceived transgressions against the tribal laws.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The underlined part "atone at" is incorrect because the verb atone is a word that takes a fixed preposition. In English grammar, when you want to express the act of making amends or compensation for a mistake, sin, or crime, you must use atone for. Fixed Preposition: Certain verbs are always followed by specific prepositions to convey a particular meaning. Atone for - प्रायश्चित करना / हर्जाना भरना - To make amends or reparation for an offense or a crime. Example - The criminal worked in the community to atone for his past misdeeds.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence.
options: One of the most common mistake made by beginners is ignoring the basic rules of grammar.
It is high time that the government takes immediate action to control the rising inflation.
Each of the participants who was selected for the final round are exceptionally talented.
Five kilometers is a long distance for someone who has not exercised in several months.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). Five kilometers is a long distance for someone who has not exercised in several months. 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct because it follows the rule of Units of Measurement. When a plural noun (Five kilometers) is used to denote a specific distance, weight, height, or amount of money as a single collective unit, it must take a singular verb (is). Explanation for other wrong options: Option A: Error in Noun Number after "One of". The phrase "One of the" must always be followed by a plural noun because it indicates choosing one from a group of many. So replace "mistake" with "mistakes." Option B: Error in Verb Tense with "It is high time". When the phrase "It is high time" is followed by a subject, the verb must be in the Simple Past Tense (V2). This is used to express that an action is already overdue. So, replace "takes" with "took." Option C: Error in Subject-Verb Agreement with "Each of". The phrase "Each of" is always singular in nature, regardless of the plural noun that follows it. Therefore, the main verb of the sentence must be singular. So, Replace "are" with "is."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (4). The correctly spelt word is Kindergarten. 1. Kindergarten (Correct) बालवाड़ी / बच्चों का स्कूल A school or class for young children, usually the first year of formal education. Example: My younger brother is excited to start kindergarten next week. Meaning and Spelling of other words: 2. Correct spelling: Legionnaire - सिपाही (विशेष रूप से फ्रेंच फॉरेन लीजन का) - A member of a legion (specifically the French Foreign Legion). Example: The old man was a decorated legionnaire who served for twenty years. 3. Correct spelling: Machiavellian - कुटिल / चालाकी भरा - Cunning, scheming, and unscrupulous, especially in politics. Example: The politician's Machiavellian tactics helped him win the election but lost him his friends. 4. Correct spelling: Fulfilment - संतुष्टि / पूर्णता - The achievement of something desired, promised, or predicted. Example: Winning the trophy brought him a great sense of fulfilment. (Note: Fulfillment is correct in US English, but SSC often follows British English "Fulfilment". However, in your list, Kindergarten is the most standard correct form. If we look at the provided option "Fulfillment", it is the US spelling).

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. After spending an hour at the beach, we finally got out and dried us.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) 📌 Explanation - The correct option is "Got out and dried ourselves" because the subject of the sentence is "we." In English grammar, when the subject and the object of a verb are the same person or group, we must use a Reflexive Pronoun. Since the subject is "we," the reflexive object must be "ourselves." Grammatical concept: * Reflexive Pronouns: These are used when the action performed by the subject is directed back toward the subject itself. Rule: Subject (We) ____ Reflexive Object (Ourselves). Incorrect usage of "Us": Using "us" (an objective pronoun) implies that "we" dried a different group of people, which does not make sense in the context of coming out of the water. Phrasal Verb "Got out": This is the appropriate phrasal verb to describe exiting a body of water (like the sea or a pool).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - I first knew him as an impecunious student living in a tiny apartment.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). Impecunious - निर्धन / दरिद्र - Having little or no money. Example - He grew up in an impecunious household but worked hard to become a successful lawyer. Correct Option (B) - Indigent - गरीब / मोहताज - Very poor, lacking the basic necessities of life. Example - The state provides free legal aid to indigent defendants who cannot afford an attorney. Explanation of other 3 options: Intelligent- बुद्धिमान - Having or showing high mental capacity or quickness of mind. Example: She is an intelligent student who can solve complex math problems in seconds. Impervious - अभेद्य / अप्रभावित - Not allowing fluid to pass through; unable to be affected or disturbed. Example: She sat there, seemingly impervious to the insults being shouted at her. Impertinent - गुस्ताख़ / ढीट - Not showing proper respect; rude or irrelevant. Example: The teacher punished the student for making impertinent remarks during the lecture.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. SQUIRM
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). Squirm - छटपटाना / तड़पना - To twist the body from side to side, usually because of nervousness, pain, or embarrassment. Example - The little boy began to squirm in his seat because the movie was too long. Correct Option (d) - Steady - स्थिर / अचल - Firmly fixed, supported, or balanced; not shaking or moving. Example - You need a steady hand to perform such a delicate surgical operation. Explanation of other 3 options: Wriggle - छटपटाना / टेढ़े-मेढ़े चलना - To twist and turn with quick writhing movements (Synonym of Squirm). Example: The child tried to wriggle out of his father's tight grip. Fidget - बेचैनी से हिलना-डुलना - To make small movements, especially of the hands and feet, through nervousness or impatience. Example: Stop fidgeting with your pen and focus on the exam paper! Shiver - काँपना / थरथराना - To shake slightly and uncontrollably as a result of being cold, frightened, or excited. Example: I saw the poor puppy shiver in the cold rain and brought him inside.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Despite his reputation for being a ________ businessman who rarely let emotions dictate his investments, he felt a sudden pang of guilt while closing the deal that would displace hundreds of local artisans.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - Perspicacious is the most appropriate fit because it describes someone who has a ready insight into and understanding of things—essentially a "shrewd" or "keen" businessman. In the context of investments and not letting emotions dictate terms, it highlights his sharp, analytical, and discerning nature. Meanings and explanation of other options: Moronic - अत्यंत मूर्ख - Very foolish or stupid Example: It was a moronic decision to leave the car unlocked in such a high-crime area. Puissant - शक्तिशाली / सामर्थ्यवान - Having great power or influence. Hysteric - अति-उत्तेजित / विक्षिप्त- Affected by or deriving from wild, uncontrolled emotion. (This is the opposite of the businessman described, who "rarely let emotions dictate" his actions). Example: The fans became hysteric when the pop star finally walked onto the stage.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. To play ducks and drakes
options: To act in a manner that is consistently inconsistent or unreliable.
To handle a delicate situation with extreme caution and precision.
To achieve success through unethical and deceitful practices.
To squander or spend money in a reckless and extravagant way.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is : option (4). Hindi Meaning: पैसे पानी की तरह बहाना / लापरवाही से उड़ाना English Meaning: To behave recklessly; to squander or spend money in a foolish or extravagant way. Example: After inheriting a fortune from his uncle, he began to play ducks and drakes with his wealth and soon became penniless. 📌 Origin of the Idiom - The idiom originates from a very old game called "Ducks and Drakes" (also known as stone-skipping), where players throw a flat stone across the surface of the water to see how many times it can bounce. Just as the stone is "thrown away" into the water for a moment's amusement, the idiom evolved to describe someone who "throws away" their resources or money carelessly.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: Despite the fact that the international humanitarian organization had
received a substantial funding from several affluent nations and private
donors to mitigate the effects of the famine, the crisis continued to escalate.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (2). 👉 Replace "a substantial funding" with "substantial funding" 📌 Error concept: The error lies in the use of the indefinite article 'a' with the uncountable noun 'funding'. Grammatical concept: * Uncountable Nouns: Nouns like funding, information, advice, furniture, luggage, and equipment are uncountable. They do not take the indefinite article 'a' or 'an' before them. Adjectives with Uncountable Nouns: While you can use an adjective like "substantial" to describe the noun, the rule for the article remains the same. You can say "substantial funding" or "the substantial funding" (if specific), but never "a funding". Exception: If you want to use a count mask, you would say "a substantial amount of funding."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. KILTER
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). Kilter - ठीक हालत / संतुलन - Good working order; a state of harmony or balance (usually used in the phrase "out of kilter"). Example - After the power surge, the delicate instruments in the laboratory were completely out of kilter. Correct Option (A) - Equilibrium - संतुलन / साम्यावस्था A state in which opposing forces or influences are balanced. Example - Yoga helps me maintain my emotional equilibrium even during stressful work weeks. Explanation of other 3 options: Disarray - अव्यवस्था / गड़बड़ी - A state of organization or untidiness. Example: The sudden resignation of the CEO left the entire company in a state of disarray. Delineate - वर्णन करना / रूपरेखा बनाना - To describe or portray something precisely; to indicate the exact position of a boundary. Example: The main objectives of the project are clearly delineated in the first chapter of the report. Tutelage - संरक्षण / शिक्षण - Protection of or authority over someone or something; guardianship or instruction. Example: Under the tutelage of the master chef, the young apprentice quickly learned the secrets of French cuisine.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The architect designed a sophisticated skyscraper that blended cutting-edge technology with timeless aesthetics, standing as a marvel of modern engineering.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (4) Sophisticated - परिष्कृत / जटिल और उन्नत - Having a high degree of complexity and refinement; advanced. Example - The new security system is highly sophisticated and uses biometric data for access. Correct Option (4) - Tawdry - भड़कीला और सस्ता / घटिया - Showy but cheap and of poor quality; lacking in dignity or taste. Example - The historic building was ruined by tawdry neon signs and cheap plastic decorations. Explanation of other 3 options: Elegant - शिष्ट / सुंदर - Graceful and stylish in appearance or manner (Synonym of sophisticated). Example: She looked very elegant in her simple black silk dress. Intricate - जटिल / पेचीदा - Very complicated or detailed - Example: The watchmaker spent months assembling the intricate parts of the timepiece. Infatuation - सम्मोहन / आसक्ति - An intense but short-lived passion or admiration for someone or something. Example: His sudden interest in heavy metal music was just a passing teenage infatuation.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The philosopher's tenure at the institution was legendary.
options: He lost tenure due to academic misconduct.
The lease agreement defines tenure by clause.
The sculptor captured the tenure of pain in marble.
Colonial tenure laws were challenged by activists.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation: In the given sentence, Tenure means– the holding of a position or office (especially a permanent academic appointment). A, B, D → Here in these sentences, tenure is used in the sense of holding a position, land, or rights, same meaning as the given sentence. C. “The sculptor captured the tenure of pain in marble.” → Here, tenure refers to the duration or persistence of an abstract state (pain), not an office or legal holding, so the meaning is different. So, the correct homonym is option (C).

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A speech or piece of writing giving advice on the correct way to behave.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) Homily (Correct) - धर्मोपदेश / उपदेश - A religious discourse that is intended primarily for spiritual edification rather than doctrinal instruction; a tedious moralizing lecture. Example: The priest delivered a short homily on the importance of forgiveness during the Sunday service. A) Panegyric - गुणगान / स्तुति - A public speech or published text in praise of someone or something. Example: The journalist wrote a glowing panegyric on the retired athlete’s illustrious career. C) Diatribe - निंदात्मक भाषण / कटु आलोचना - A forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something. Example: The candidate launched into a long diatribe against the government's economic policies. D) Eulogy - प्रशंसा भाषण (आमतौर पर मृतक के लिए) - A speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly, typically someone who has just died. Example: His best friend delivered a moving eulogy at the funeral service, sharing stories of their childhood.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Pound of flesh
options: A harsh demand for rightful payment
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is: option (C). (C) Hindi Meaning: कठोर शर्त / निर्दयी मांग (जो कानूनी रूप से सही हो पर नैतिक रूप से गलत) English Meaning: An unreasonable or harsh demand for something that is legally owed, regardless of how much suffering it causes the debtor. Example: The bank is demanding its pound of flesh by seizing the widow's house to pay off her late husband's small debt. 📌 Origin of the Idiom - The idiom originates from William Shakespeare's play, The Merchant of Venice. In the play, the moneylender Shylock demands a "pound of flesh" from the merchant Antonio as a penalty if he fails to repay a loan. Shylock insists on the literal flesh even though it would kill Antonio, simply because it was written in the legal contract.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. The use of light and shadow to create the illusion of depth or three-dimensionality in a two-dimensional work of art.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). Chiaroscuro (Correct): प्रकाश और छाया का खेल (चित्रकला में) - The treatment of light and shade in drawing and painting; an effect of contrasted light and shadow. Example: Rembrandt is famous for his mastery of chiaroscuro, using deep shadows to make his subjects look lifelike. a.) Martinet - सख्त अनुशासक - A strict disciplinarian, especially in the armed forces. Example: The new drill sergeant was a real martinet, insisting that every button be perfectly polished. b.) Stevedore - जहाज़ी कुली / जहाज़ से माल उतारने वाला A person employed at a dock to load and unload ships. Example: The stevedores worked through the night to ensure the cargo ship was ready to sail by dawn. d.) Coffle - कैदियों या गुलामों का कारवां - A line of animals, prisoners, or slaves chained together in a row. Example: The historical records described a coffle of captives being led across the desert.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. (P) This rapid urbanization, while a sign of economic progress, often outpaces the development of essential infrastructure like sanitation and public transport. (Q) Millions of people migrate from rural areas to metropolitan hubs every year in search of better employment opportunities and modern lifestyles. (R) Consequently, these cities face a daunting challenge in maintaining a sustainable quality of life for their burgeoning populations. (S) India is currently witnessing one of the largest internal migrations in its history, driven by the shift from agrarian to industrial economies.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct option is (A) SQPR. 📌 Explanation - Step 1 (The Opening): Sentence S is the best introductory sentence. It introduces the broad theme of the passage: the massive internal migration currently happening in India. Step 2 (The Elaboration): Sentence Q follows S perfectly. While S mentions the fact that migration is happening, Q provides the specific details—millions of people moving from rural areas to metropolitan hubs for jobs and lifestyle. Step 3 (The Connective): Sentence P starts with "This rapid urbanization." This phrase is a direct reference to the "migration to metropolitan hubs" described in sentence Q. It explains the consequence of that movement (infrastructure being outpaced). Step 4 (The Conclusion): Sentence R starts with the transition word "Consequently." It summarizes the end result of the process described in S, Q, and P: the challenge of maintaining a sustainable quality of life. Final Paragraph: India is currently witnessing one of the largest internal migrations in its history, driven by the shift from agrarian to industrial economies. Millions of people migrate from rural areas to metropolitan hubs every year in search of better employment opportunities and modern lifestyles. This rapid urbanization, while a sign of economic progress, often outpaces the development of essential infrastructure like sanitation and public transport. Consequently, these cities face a daunting challenge in maintaining a sustainable quality of life for their burgeoning populations.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The ministers were being briefed about the situation by the intelligence officers.
options: The intelligence officers briefed the ministers about the situation.
The intelligence officers had briefed the ministers.
The ministers were briefed by the intelligence officers.
The intelligence officers were briefing the ministers.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - The original sentence is in the Past Continuous Tense (Passive Voice), indicated by the structure "were being + V3". To convert it into Active Voice, we must maintain the same tense, which is the Past Continuous Tense (was/were + V{ing} ). Grammatical concept: The conversion of Past Continuous Tense follows this rule: Active voice: Subject + was/were + V{ing} + Object Passive voice: Object + was/were + being + V3 + by + Subject Analysis of all the options: Option (a) is incorrect because it is in Simple Past Tense ("briefed"). Option (b) is incorrect because it is in Past Perfect Tense ("had briefed"). Option (c) is incorrect because it is still in the Passive Voice (Simple Past). Option (d) is correct as it uses "were briefing," which matches the continuous nature of the original sentence.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The zoologist warned that the island's carrying capacity might be exceeded should invasive species proliferate unchecked.
options: The zoologist said, "The island's carrying capacity will be exceeded if invasive species proliferate unchecked."
The zoologist said, "Should invasive species have proliferated unchecked, the island's carrying capacity would be exceeded.”
The zoologist said, "The island's carrying capacity might be exceeded should invasive species proliferate unchecked.”
The zoologist said, "If invasive species proliferated unchecked, the carrying capacity had been exceeded.”
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - This question tests your ability to convert Indirect Speech back into Direct Speech when a conditional/modal structure is used. In the original sentence, the reporting verb is "warned" (Past Tense) and the modal used is "might." * Option (c) is the most accurate because it perfectly mirrors the formal conditional structure "should... proliferate" (which means "if... proliferate") and retains the modal "might." Option (a) is incorrect because it changes "might" to "will," altering the degree of possibility. Option (b) and (d) change the tenses to Perfect/Past forms which do not match the original statement's meaning. Grammatical concept: 1. Modal Consistency: In Direct/Indirect speech, might usually remains might in both forms if it expresses a possibility. If "may" were in the direct speech, it would change to "might" in the indirect; however, since the original indirect sentence uses "might," the most direct and accurate reflection is to keep "might" unless "may" is provided in a contextually superior option. 2. Formal Conditionals (Inversion with 'Should'): The use of "should" at the beginning of a clause is a formal way of saying "if." * Direct: "Should it rain, the match will be cancelled." * Meaning: "If it should rain..." 3. Reporting Verb: The word "warned" in indirect speech is a contextual replacement for "said" in direct speech to convey the tone of the message.

questions.question: Traditionally, the study of history has had fixed boundaries and focal points—periods, countries, dramatic events, and great leaders. It also has had clear and firm notions of scholarly procedure: how one inquires ____(1)____ a historical problem, how one presents and documents one's findings, what constitutes admissible and ____(2)____ proof. Anyone who has followed recent historical literature can testify to the revolution that is taking place in historical studies. The old questions "What happened?" and "How did it happen?" have given way to the question "Why did it happen?". Prominent among the methods used to answer the question "Why" is psychoanalysis, and its use ____(3)____ rise to psychohistory. Psychohistory does not ____(4)____ use psychological explanations in historical contexts. Historians have always used such explanations when they were appropriate and when there was sufficient evidence for them. But this pragmatic use of psychology is not what psych-historians intend. They are committed, not just to psychology in general, but to Freudian psychoanalysis. This commitment ____(5)____ a commitment to history as historians have always understood it. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) into. 💢 Explanation - The verb "inquire" is followed by the preposition "into" when it means to investigate or examine the facts of a problem or situation. Why others are wrong: "Inquire about" is used for general information (e.g., inquiring about a price), but for a "historical problem" (investigation), "into" is the standard scholarly collocation.

questions.question: Traditionally, the study of history has had fixed boundaries and focal points—periods, countries, dramatic events, and great leaders. It also has had clear and firm notions of scholarly procedure: how one inquires ____(1)____ a historical problem, how one presents and documents one's findings, what constitutes admissible and ____(2)____ proof. Anyone who has followed recent historical literature can testify to the revolution that is taking place in historical studies. The old questions "What happened?" and "How did it happen?" have given way to the question "Why did it happen?". Prominent among the methods used to answer the question "Why" is psychoanalysis, and its use ____(3)____ rise to psychohistory. Psychohistory does not ____(4)____ use psychological explanations in historical contexts. Historians have always used such explanations when they were appropriate and when there was sufficient evidence for them. But this pragmatic use of psychology is not what psych-historians intend. They are committed, not just to psychology in general, but to Freudian psychoanalysis. This commitment ____(5)____ a commitment to history as historians have always understood it. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) adequate. 💢 Explanation - The sentence discusses "scholarly procedure" and what constitutes valid proof. "Adequate" sufficient /satisfactory) fits the parallel structure with "admissible" (acceptable). Why others are wrong: "Inadequate" (not enough), "Ambiguous" (unclear), and "Cryptic" (mysterious) all carry negative or unfitting meanings for a formal definition of proof.

questions.question: Traditionally, the study of history has had fixed boundaries and focal points—periods, countries, dramatic events, and great leaders. It also has had clear and firm notions of scholarly procedure: how one inquires ____(1)____ a historical problem, how one presents and documents one's findings, what constitutes admissible and ____(2)____ proof. Anyone who has followed recent historical literature can testify to the revolution that is taking place in historical studies. The old questions "What happened?" and "How did it happen?" have given way to the question "Why did it happen?". Prominent among the methods used to answer the question "Why" is psychoanalysis, and its use ____(3)____ rise to psychohistory. Psychohistory does not ____(4)____ use psychological explanations in historical contexts. Historians have always used such explanations when they were appropriate and when there was sufficient evidence for them. But this pragmatic use of psychology is not what psych-historians intend. They are committed, not just to psychology in general, but to Freudian psychoanalysis. This commitment ____(5)____ a commitment to history as historians have always understood it. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) gave. 💢 Explanation - The passage describes a transition that occurred in the past ("Where history once was... it is now..."). The specific use of psychoanalysis resulted in the birth of a new field. The phrase "gave rise to" is the idiomatic expression for "resulted in." Why others are wrong: While "has given" (Present Perfect) is tempting, the passage describes the historical shift in methods using simple past/present contrasts. "Gave" is the most direct fit for the origin of the term.

questions.question: Traditionally, the study of history has had fixed boundaries and focal points—periods, countries, dramatic events, and great leaders. It also has had clear and firm notions of scholarly procedure: how one inquires ____(1)____ a historical problem, how one presents and documents one's findings, what constitutes admissible and ____(2)____ proof. Anyone who has followed recent historical literature can testify to the revolution that is taking place in historical studies. The old questions "What happened?" and "How did it happen?" have given way to the question "Why did it happen?". Prominent among the methods used to answer the question "Why" is psychoanalysis, and its use ____(3)____ rise to psychohistory. Psychohistory does not ____(4)____ use psychological explanations in historical contexts. Historians have always used such explanations when they were appropriate and when there was sufficient evidence for them. But this pragmatic use of psychology is not what psych-historians intend. They are committed, not just to psychology in general, but to Freudian psychoanalysis. This commitment ____(5)____ a commitment to history as historians have always understood it. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) merely. 💢 Explanation - The author is making a distinction. He says psychohistory does not "merely" (just/only) use psychology, but rather a specific type (Freudian). Why others are wrong: "Seldom" (rarely) and "Hardly" (barely) contradict the passage, as psychohistorians do use it. "Exclusively" is too strong, as they use other tools, but the contrast here is between "general" use and "specific" Freudian use.

questions.question: Traditionally, the study of history has had fixed boundaries and focal points—periods, countries, dramatic events, and great leaders. It also has had clear and firm notions of scholarly procedure: how one inquires ____(1)____ a historical problem, how one presents and documents one's findings, what constitutes admissible and ____(2)____ proof. Anyone who has followed recent historical literature can testify to the revolution that is taking place in historical studies. The old questions "What happened?" and "How did it happen?" have given way to the question "Why did it happen?". Prominent among the methods used to answer the question "Why" is psychoanalysis, and its use ____(3)____ rise to psychohistory. Psychohistory does not ____(4)____ use psychological explanations in historical contexts. Historians have always used such explanations when they were appropriate and when there was sufficient evidence for them. But this pragmatic use of psychology is not what psych-historians intend. They are committed, not just to psychology in general, but to Freudian psychoanalysis. This commitment ____(5)____ a commitment to history as historians have always understood it. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) precludes. 💢 Explanation - "Precludes" means to prevent from happening or to make impossible. The author suggests that a total commitment to Freudian psychoanalysis precludes (rules out) a commitment to history as traditionally understood, because the two approaches are fundamentally different. Why others are wrong: "Precedes" (comes before), "Accentuates" (highlights), and "Provides" (gives) do not capture the sense of conflict or mutual exclusivity between the old and new methods described in the text.

questions.question: Which of the following is a hallmark symptom of Kwashiorkor?
options: Swollen abdomen due to fluid retention
questions.explanation: Kwashiorkor is a form of protein malnutrition mainly seen in children. Its most characteristic feature is edema (fluid retention), which causes a swollen abdomen and puffy appearance.

questions.question: Environmental Performance Index (EPI) 2025 released by which organizations?
questions.explanation: The Environmental Performance Index (EPI) is jointly released by Yale University and Columbia University. It ranks countries based on environmental health and ecosystem vitality. The United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) supports environmental research and data, but it is not the main publisher of the EPI.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment granted full statehood to Goa and inserted Article 371I in the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The 56th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1987 granted statehood to Goa and inserted Article 371-I, in the Constitution of India. 53rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1986: It gave statehood to Mizoram and inserted Article 371G. 55th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1986: It relates to Arunachal Pradesh and inserted Article 371H. 63rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1989: It repealed the 59th Amendment related to emergency provisions in Punjab.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about movements led by Mahatma Gandhi: 1. The Civil Disobedience Movement included the boycott of foreign goods and refusal to pay taxes. 2. The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in response to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre and the Rowlatt Act. 3. Gandhi supported violent methods during the Quit India Movement due to urgency. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct. The Civil Disobedience Movement involved defying laws, including refusal to pay taxes and boycott of foreign goods. Statement 2: Correct. The Non-Cooperation Movement (1920) was launched in response to the Rowlatt Act and the Jallianwala Bagh massacre. Statement 3: Incorrect. Gandhi strictly followed non-violence and never supported violent methods, even during the Quit India Movement.

questions.question: Who won the men’s singles title at the World Table Tennis Championships 2025?
questions.explanation: Wang Chuqin won the men’s singles title at the World Table Tennis Championships 2025. The WTT Finals have been hosted in Singapore. It is a major event under the World Table Tennis (WTT) series, featuring top-ranked players. China national table tennis team dominated the World Table Tennis Championships 2025, continuing its long-standing supremacy in the sport.

questions.question: Pandwani dance is related to which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Pandwani is a traditional folk storytelling dance form of Chhattisgarh, based on stories from the Mahabharata. It is performed in a narrative style with music. Famous Pandwani artist is Teejan Bai, who popularized this art form.

questions.question: The jet engine was invented by :
questions.explanation: The modern jet engine was invented by Frank Whittle in the 1930s. James Watt: Known for improvements in the steam engine. Nikola Tesla: Known for contributions to electricity and AC systems. Rudolf Diesel: Invented the diesel engine.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Meenakshi Amman Temple : 1. The early structure of the temple is attributed to King Kulasekara Pandya of the Pandya dynasty. 2. The temple is located on the southern bank of the Vaigai River and is dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareswarar 3. The present structure of the temple was largely rebuilt during the Nayak rule, especially under Tirumala Nayak in the 17th century. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The early structure and founding of the temple are traditionally attributed to King Kulasekara Pandya of the Pandya dynasty, with historical roots dating back to at least the 6th century BCE or the 1st century CE. Statement 2 is correct: The temple is situated on the southern bank of the Vaigai River in Madurai, Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi (an avatar of Parvati) and her consort Lord Sundareswarar (a form of Shiva). Statement 3 is correct: After being plundered in the 14th century, the temple was extensively rebuilt and expanded during the Nayak rule (16th–17th centuries). Tirumala Nayak (reigned 1623–1655) is credited with significantly expanding it into the massive complex seen today.

questions.question: Cobalt (Co) belongs to which group of the Modern Periodic Table?
questions.explanation: Cobalt (Co) is primarily located in Group 9 of the periodic table, which is part of the d-block transition metals in Period 4.

questions.question: Khubani (Apricot Blossom) Festival 2025 is primarily celebrated in which of the following Union Territories or state?
questions.explanation: The Khubani or Apricot Blossom Festival is celebrated in Ladakh during spring to mark the blooming of apricot trees and to promote tourism and local culture.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India deals with the provision of equal pay for equal work for both men and women?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) of the Constitution of India directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women. Article 38: Deals with promotion of welfare of the people and social order Article 42: Deals with provision for just and humane conditions of work and maternity relief. Article 44: Deals with Uniform Civil Code.

questions.question: Match the following: List I (Ocean Current) List II (Characteristic) A) Gulf Stream 1) Cold current flowing from Arctic region along eastern Canada B) Kuroshio Current 2) Warm current flowing along the eastern coast of Japan C) Agulhas Current 3) Warm current flowing along the southeastern coast of Africa D) Labrador Current 4) Warm current originating in the Gulf of Mexico and moving towards Europe
questions.explanation: Gulf Stream → Warm current originating in the Gulf of Mexico and flowing towards Europe. Kuroshio Current → Warm current along the eastern coast of Japan. Agulhas Current → Warm current along the southeastern coast of Africa. Labrador Current → Cold current flowing from the Arctic along eastern Canada.

questions.question: Consider the following statements : 1. Indira Soura Giri Jal Vikasam Yojana is a scheme launched by the Kerla government. 2. It focuses on providing solar-powered irrigation facilities and water development for tribal farmers, especially in hilly (giri) regions. Which of the above statement is correct?
questions.explanation: Indira Soura Giri Jal Vikasam Yojana is a scheme launched by the Telangana government. It focuses on providing solar-powered irrigation facilities and water development for tribal farmers, especially in hilly (giri) regions

questions.question: Which of the following monetary policy tools requires banks to maintain a certain percentage of their Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) in the form of liquid assets such as cash, gold, or government securities?
questions.explanation: Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) requires banks to maintain a fixed percentage of their NDTL in liquid assets like cash, gold, or government securities. Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR): Banks must keep a percentage of deposits as cash with the Reserve Bank of India. Bank Rate: It is the rate at which the RBI lends to commercial banks without collateral. Repo Rate: It is the rate at which the RBI lends to banks against government securities.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. The Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha is appointed by the Speaker of Lok Sabha. 2. The President of India can summon or prorogue either House of Parliament, but cannot dissolve the Rajya Sabha. 3. Delegated legislation made by administrative authorities can not be challenged in a court of low. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Incorrect. The Deputy Speaker is elected by the members of the Lok Sabha, not appointed by the Speaker. Statement 2: Correct. The President can summon and prorogue either House and dissolve only the Lok Sabha. The Rajya Sabha is a permanent body. Statement 3: Incorrect. Delegated legislation can be challenged in a court of law if it is unconstitutional or exceeds the powers delegated by the parent Act.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Kho Kho: 1. Kho Kho is a traditional Indian tag game played between two teams of 12 players each, with 9 players on the field at a time. 2. A total of 23 countries participated in the first-ever Kho Kho World Cup 2025 held in New Delhi, India. 3. In the tournament, the best player awards went to Pratik Waikar (men’s section) and Priyanka Ingle (women’s section), recognising their outstanding performances. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Kho Kho is a traditional Indian tag game played between two teams of 12 players each, with 9 players on the field at a time. The 2025 Kho Kho World Cup was the inaugural edition of the tournament held at the Indira Gandhi Arena in New Delhi India won both men’s and women’s titles, defeating Nepal. A total of 23 countries participated in the first-ever Kho Kho World Cup 2025 held in New Delhi, India. The event featured 20 men’s teams and 19 women’s teams from six continents. In the tournament, the best player awards went to Pratik Waikar (men’s section) and Priyanka Ingle (women’s section), recognising their outstanding performances

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the System of Rice Intensification (SRI)?
options: Transplanting older seedlings at close spacing
Frequent flooding of fields to maintain high water levels
Transplanting young seedlings with wider spacing and reducing water usage
Use of only high-yielding rice varieties without changing management practices
questions.explanation: SRI is a method of rice cultivation that: Uses young seedlings (8–12 days old) instead of older ones Plants them at wider spacing to improve root growth and aeration Reduces water use compared to traditional flooded methods Encourages organic fertilization and better soil management

questions.question: Which of the following measures is generally used to control demand-pull inflation?
options: Reducing taxes and increasing government spending
Increasing interest rates and reducing money supply
questions.explanation: Demand-pull inflation occurs when aggregate demand exceeds aggregate supply. To control it, the central bank may increase interest rates and reduce money supply.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Keoladeo Ghana National Park: 1. Keoladeo Ghana National Park, located in Bharatpur, Rajasthan, is a designated Ramsar Wetland of International Importance and is famous for its migratory birds. 2. The wetland is a natural freshwater lake formed by tectonic activity and has no connection to human-made water management systems. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct. The Keoladeo Ghana National Park is a Ramsar site and a world-famous habitat for migratory birds. Statement 2: Incorrect. The wetland is largely human-made, created by the flooding of the Ajan Bund and maintained historically through irrigation systems, not purely by natural tectonic activity.

questions.question: The SpaDeX mission by the Indian Space Research Organisation successfully demonstrated which key space technology?
options: Autonomous satellite formation flying and docking maneuvers in low Earth orbit
Mars orbit insertion with precision trajectory correction
Venus atmospheric probe deployment and sample collection
Extraction of water ice from near-Earth asteroids using robotic arms
questions.explanation: The SpaDeX mission involved two satellites, SDX01 and SDX02, which demonstrated autonomous in-space rendezvous, docking, and undocking. This capability is crucial for future applications like space stations, orbital maintenance, and inter-satellite transfers.

questions.question: According to the Census of India 2011, which Union Territory had the highest literacy rate, and which had the lowest literacy rate?
options: Lakshadweep highest, Dadra and Nagar Haveli lowest
Lakshadweep highest, Daman and Diu lowest lowest
Andaman and Nicobar highest, Daman and Diu lowest
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep recorded the highest literacy rate among Union Territories at around 91.85 percent in Census 2011, while Dadra and Nagar Haveli had the lowest literacy rate among Union Territories at around 77.7 percent.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the second Lokpal of India in 2024?
questions.explanation: The second Lokpal, appointed in 2024, is Justice Ajay Manikrao Khanwilkar. The Lokpal is the head of the anti-corruption body in India, tasked with investigating corruption cases against public officials. First Lokpal: Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose appointed in 2019 The Chairperson and members of Lokpal are appointed by the President of India based on the recommendation of a committee consisting of the Prime Minister, Speaker of Lok Sabha, Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha, Chief Justice of India or a Supreme Court nominee, and Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the 17th BRICS Summit held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil in 2025: 1. The summit was held under the theme “Strengthening Global South Cooperation toward More Inclusive and Sustainable Governance”. 2. Several new partner countries such as Belarus, Malaysia, and Vietnam were welcomed at the summit. 3. Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended the summit and spoke about marginalization of the Global South. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: The 2025 BRICS Summit theme focused on cooperation and inclusive governance among Global South nations. Statement 2: Several nations including Belarus, Malaysia, and Vietnam were recognised as BRICS partner countries. Statement 3: Prime Minister Modi spoke at the summit on issues facing the Global South and called for comprehensive reforms

questions.question: The Gupta Empire was divided into provinces called ______ and each administered by a governor called ______.
questions.explanation: The Gupta Empire was divided into Bhuktis (provinces) for administrative convenience. Each Bhukti was governed by an Uparika (provincial governor) appointed by the emperor.

questions.question: During photosynthesis, plants convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen. Which of the following is the primary site of this process?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis occurs in the chloroplasts, specifically in the thylakoid membranes where light-dependent reactions take place.

questions.question: A container has a 250-litre mixture of acid and water in the ratio 4:1. 50 litres is removed and replaced with water. Then 50 litres is removed again and replaced with pure acid. What is the final percentage of acid?
questions.explanation: Total volume = 250 L Quantities of: Acid = (4/5) × 250 = 200 L And, Water = (1/5) × 250 = 50 L After 1st replacement: Acid removed = (20/100) × 200 = 40 L i.e. Remaining Acid = 200 - 40 = 160 L After 2nd replacement: Acid removed = (20/100) × 160 = 32 L But 50 L acid is added, so Final quantity of acid = (160 - 32) + 50 = 178 L Final Percentage of acid = (178 / 250) × 100 = 71.2%

questions.question: If secA – tanA = 1/√3, then find secA + tanA.
questions.explanation: Given: secA – tanA = 1/√3 …..(A) Using the identity: sec² A - tan² A = 1 → (sec A - tan A)(sec A + tan A) = 1 We get, secA + tanA = √3 …..(B) On solving both (A) and (B), we get sec A = 2/√3, and tan A = 1/√3 thus, secA + tanA = (2+1)/√3 = 3/√3 = √3

questions.question: Simplify the following:
questions.explanation: Let x = → x = √(20 + x) On squaring both sides: x² = 20 + x → x² - x - 20 = 0 → (x - 5)(x + 4) = 0 → x = 5

questions.question: There are 8 consecutive integers and 5 consecutive integers. The average of the 8 integers is 3 less than the average of the 5 integers. The sum of the 5 integers is 11 less than the sum of the 8 integers. Find the average of the 8 integers.
questions.explanation: Let the average of the 8 integers = x, and average of the 5 integers = y ATQ: x = y - 3 => y = x + 3 …..(A) using, Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations 5y = 8x – 11 …..(B) On solving (A) and (B), we get: x = 26/3 = 8.667 and y = 11.667 Hence, the average of the 8 integers is 8.67.

questions.question: A sum becomes Rs. 11,760 in 2 years at r% simple interest. If the interest rate had been (r + 13)% p.a., the amount would have been Rs. 13,320. Find the principal.
questions.explanation: SI = PRT/100, and Amount (A) = P + SI Let, P be the required principal. A1 = P + P*r*2/100 = 11,760 A2 = P + P*(r+13)*2/100 = 13,320 ATQ: A2 – A1 = ₹ 1,560 → P*13*2/100 = ₹ 1,560 → P = ₹ 6,000

questions.question: A water filter tank is 1 m wide, 0.8 m deep, and 1.5 m long. It takes 2.5 hours to fill it. At what rate is water being filled (in liters per minute)?
questions.explanation: Volume of the tank (in m³) = Length × Width × Depth Volume = 1.5 m × 1 m × 0.8 m = 1.2 m³ = 1200 liters (1 m³ = 1000 liters) Filling Rate = (Total Volume)/(Total Time) = (1200 liters)/(150 minutes) → Rate = 8 liters/min

questions.question: If two circles of radius r1 and r2 have exactly one common tangent, what is the distance between their centers?
questions.explanation: As we know: Four Common Tangents: Circles are separate (no intersection). The distance between centers is greater than the sum of their radii: d>( r1+ r2) Three Common Tangents: Circles touch externally. The distance between centers is exactly the sum of their radii: d= r1+ r2 Two Common Tangents: Circles intersect at two points. The distance between centers is less than the sum but greater than the difference: |r1- r2| r1+ r2) One Common Tangent: This occurs only when one circle touches the other internally. At the point where they touch, they share a single tangent line. In this case, the distance between their centers is exactly the difference between their radii: d=|r1- r2| Zero Common Tangents: One circle is entirely inside the other without touching. The distance between centers is less than the difference of their radii: dr1- r2| Hence, for exactly one common tangent, the circles must touch internally, making the distance between their centers |r1- r2|.

questions.question: A seller offers a combo deal: "Buy 5 shirts for the price of 2". If each shirt costs him Rs. 100, what is the effective loss percentage?
questions.explanation: Let C and S be the cost and selling prices respectively. Here, C = Rs. 100/shirt i.e. total C (for the shopkeeper) = Rs. 5*100 = Rs. 500 ATQ: As per the offer: "Buy 5 for the price of 2" → The customer only pays for 2 shirts i.e. total S (for the shopkeeper) = Rs. 2*100 = Rs. 200 Loss (L) = C-S = Rs. 300 thus, Loss percentage = (L/C)×100% = (300/500)×100% L% = 60%

questions.question: If A, B and C are three numbers, such that the L.C.M. of A and B is B and the L.C.M. of B and C is C, then find the product of the L.C.M. (A, B and C), third power of A and twice of B.
questions.explanation: Given: L.C.M. (A, B) = B, it implies that B is a multiple of A or B=k1×A. Given: L.C.M. (B, C) = C, it implies that C is a multiple of B or C=k2×B. → C = k3×A Therefore, C is a multiple of both A and B, and hence, the L.C.M. (A, B, C) = C. Thus, The required product of the L.C.M. (A, B and C), third power of A and twice of B = C×A3×2B = 2A3×B×C

questions.question: If x + 1/x= -1. Find the value of the following:
In how many different ways can the letters of the word ‘MOBILE’ be arranged in such a way that the vowels occupy only the odd positions?
questions.explanation: There are 6 letters in the given word, out of which there are 3 vowels and 3 consonants. Let us mark these positions as under: (1) (2) (3) (4) (5) (6) Now, 3 vowels can be placed at any of the three places, marked 1, 3, 5. Number of ways of arranging the vowels = 3P3 = 3!/0! = 6 Also, the 3 consonants can be arranged at the remaining 3 positions. Number of ways of these arrangements = 3P3 = 3!/0! = 6 (0! = 1) Total number of ways = (6 × 6) = 36

questions.question: In a 150-m race, if R gets a 15-metre head start, then he would beat S by 25 metres. If S gets a 25-metre head start in a 110-metre race, then ______ would win the race by ______ metres.
questions.explanation: Let speeds of R and S be ‘R’ and ‘S’ respectively. As we know, speed = distance/time i.e. speed ∝ distance …..(A) From Race-1: Distance covered by R = 150 - 15 = 135 m Distance covered by S = 150 - 25 = 125 m Ratio of Speeds: (R:S) = 135 : 125 = 27:25. From Race-2: S needs to cover (dS) = 110 - 25 = 85 m, while R needs to cover (dR) = 110 m From (A), we can say: dR/ dS = 27/25 Let us assume, that S wins the race, i.e. ds = 85 m, so we need to find the ‘distance travelled by R = d (say)’, in the same time. Thus, d/85 = 27/25 → d = 2295 / 25 = 91.8 m This implies, that S won the race, as R couldn’t complete the race, and our assumption was right. So, S beat R by: 110 - 91.8 = 18.2 m

questions.question: How many cubes of length 25 cm can be accommodated in a container of 5 m in length, 2 m in width and 6 m in height?
questions.explanation: No. of cubes that can be accommodated in the container = (Vol. of container)/(Vol. of each cube) = (500×200×600)/(25×25×25 ) = 3840

questions.question: A circular disc of radius 10 cm is cut into 4 equal parts. What is the area and perimeter of each part?
questions.explanation: Here, each equal part of the circle represents a quarter of a circle, with radius, r = 10 cm and circumference C. Area of each quarter of the circle = ¼ × π × r2 = 25π cm2 Perimeter of each quarter of the circle = ¼ × C + 2r = ¼ × (2×π×r) + 2r = 5π + 20 cm

questions.question: In a circle, chord AB and chord CD intersect at E such that AE : EB = 3 : 7 and CE : ED = 4 : 9. If AB = x and CD = y, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Using Intersecting Chords Theorem: AE × EB = CE × ED …..….(A) Since AB = x, thus AE = (3/10)x, EB = (7/10)x And, CD = y, thus CE = (4/13)y, ED = (9/13)y Substitute these values in (A): →(3/10)x × (7/10)x = (4/13)y × (9/13)y → (21/100)x² = (36/169)y² → x²/y² = (36/169) × (100/21) = 1200/1183

questions.question: The union of rational and irrational numbers = ?
questions.explanation: The union of rational numbers and irrational numbers is the set of Real Numbers. Rational Numbers: These are numbers that can be expressed as a ratio of two integers, eg: ½, -5, 3.2 etc. Irrational Numbers: These are numbers that cannot be written as simple fractions and have non-repeating, non-terminating decimals, eg: π, √2 etc. Hence, Real Numbers encompasses all numbers that can be found on the continuous number line.

questions.question: A software company developed 1000 licenses for a new application at a total cost of ₹5,000,000. They provided 100 licenses free to their top beta testers. For the remaining licenses, they offered a 5% discount on the market price of ₹6000 per license. Additionally, they gave 2 licenses free for every 8 licenses bought. If all 1000 licenses were distributed, what is the company's overall gain or loss percentage?
questions.explanation: Let CP and SP be the cost and selling prices respectively. Total CP = ₹50,00,000 Total licenses = Total - Beta tester freebies = 1000 - 100 = 900 Now, "Buy 8 Get 2 Free" means 8 out of 10 licenses are paid. Thus, total Paid licenses = (8/10) × 900 = 720 licenses Market Price = ₹6,000 but, Discount = 5% is given thus, SP per license = 6,000 × 0.95 = ₹5,700 Total SP = 720 × ₹5,700 = ₹41,04,000 Loss (L) = CP - SP = ₹50,00,000 - ₹41,04,000 = ₹8,96,000 Loss % = (L/C) × 100 = 17.92%

questions.question: A population falls by 18% in the first year. By what percentage must it increase in the second year so that at the end of the second year the population is 10% more than the original?
questions.explanation: Let, the initial population be 100 Population after 18% decrease i.e., new population = 100 - 18 = 82. Required population by the end of 2nd year = 110% of 100 = 110. Required increase to reach 110 is 28. Thus, Increase in percentage = (Increase / Current Population) × 100% → (28 / 82) × 100% = 34.14%

questions.question: From the top of a hill 240 metres high, the angles of depression of the top and bottom of a pole are 30° and 60°, respectively. The sum (in metres) of the height of the pole and its distance from the hill is:
questions.explanation: Let, AB be the hill of 240 m, and CD = h, which depicts the pole. Let the pole’s distance from the hill be x. In ∆ ABD, tan 60° = 240/x √3 = 240/x x = 240/√3 or 80√3 In ∆ AMC, tan 30° = AM/x 1/√3 = AM/80√3 thus, AM = 80 → h or CD = AB - AM = 160 m Thus, required sum of the height of the pole and its distance from the hill is = h + x = 160 + 80√3 = 80(2+√3) m

questions.question: Find the mode of the following data: 45, 45, 48, 47, 48, 48, 45, 47, 41, 42, 43, 44, 44, 48
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most frequently in a data set. In this list, the number 48 appears 4 times, which is more than any other number. Mode = 48

questions.question: A:B = 18:7, B:C = 5:8, C:D = 9:11. Find the ratio of A:D.
questions.explanation: To get A/D simply multiply all the ratios, i.e.,

questions.question: P borrows ₹5,00,000 from the bank which charges 19% simple interest per annum at the start of the year. He pays 7 equal monthly installments of ₹50,000 and some gold at the end of the year to pay the amount. What is the value of the gold given?
questions.explanation: SI = PRT/100, and Amount (A) = P + SI SI = (5,00,000 × 19 × 1) / 100 = ₹95,000 A = Principal (P) + SI A = 5,00,000 + 95,000 = ₹5,95,000 Total Cash Paid = 7 installments × ₹50,000 = ₹3,50,000 Thus, Value of gold = Total Amount - Total Cash Paid Value of gold = 5,95,000 - 3,50,000 = ₹2,45,000

questions.question: If the interior angle of a regular polygon is k times its exterior angle, express the number of diagonals of the polygon in terms of k.
questions.explanation: Let n be the no. of sides of the regular polygon here. As we know, the sum of interior (i) and exterior angles (e) of a polygon is 180°. i.e. i+e = 180° ..... (A) here, i = k×e ..... (B) from, A and B, we have, e(1+k) = 180° => e = 180/(1+k) We also know that, n = 360/e Here, n = 360/[180/(1+k)] = 2(k+1) Thus, no. of diagonals of the polygon = x x = [n*(n-3)] / 2 = [{2(k+1)}*{2k-1}] / 2 = (k+1)(2k-1)

questions.question: Train 'A' can cross a pole in 9 seconds, while train 'B' takes 10 seconds to cross train 'A' when they travel in opposite directions on parallel tracks. If the ratio of length of train 'A' to that of train 'B' is 3:2 and the speed of train 'A' is 60 km/h, then find the speed (in km/h) of train 'B'.
questions.explanation: Let sA and s be the speeds and lA and lB be the lengths of train A and B respectively. sA = 60 kmph, lA = 3x, lB = 2x As we know, speed = distance/time Here, distance = lengths of trains Relative speeds, when both trains move: In opposite direction: sA + s Case-1: → x = 50 thus, lA = 150 m, lB = 100 m Case-2:

questions.question: Find the value of x, if 2(x+2) + 7 = 1045 - 14
If ‘MANGO’ is coded as ‘AOGMN’, them ‘ORANGE’ is coded as?
options: A O E G R N
A O R N G E
A E O G R N
A E O G N R
questions.explanation: In MANGO, first vowels are arranged in alphabetical order and then the consonants are arranged in same manner. Hence, MANGO is coded as AOGMN

questions.question: What comes next in the Series? A2, E3, I5, O8, ?
questions.explanation: = Vowels are Following A, E, O, I, U pattern = 2, 3, 5, 8, 2+3=5 5+3=8 Hence 8+5=13 So, ? = U13

questions.question: By interchanging the given two sings which question will not correct? – and ÷ I. 60 + 20 – 4 ÷ 18 × 3 = 10 II. 40 + 80 ÷ 18 – 9 × 2 = 30

Two statements are given, followed by two conclusions numbered 1 and 2 . Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statement: No Chair is book. All books are pens. Conclusion: 1. No Chair is Pen 2. Some Pens are not chair.

Six people are seated around a circular table facing inwards. B is seated to the immediate right of D. F is seated to the immediate right of A. E is seated to immediate right of C, B and E are not seated opposite to each. C and F are not immediate neighbours. Who is third to left of A?
Statement: Either the summit will be postponed or it will be held online. The Summit will not be postponed. What conclusion is certain?
options: Summit might be held in person.
Summit will definitely be held online
questions.explanation: Since the Summit Cannot be postponed it will definitely be held online

questions.question: What was the day on 16 November 2025?
questions.explanation: Date → 16 Month code → 3 Last 2digit of year → 25 Last 2digit of year ÷4→ 6 Year code → 6 +_____ 56 56/7= Rem 0 ⇒ 0 is code for Sunday.

questions.question: If ‘GANGA’ is coded as 171417, then what is the code of ‘SINDHU’?
Roll numbers: Based on roll numbers given above, where first two digits indicate the centre ID: how many new roll numbers must centre ‘17’ register to have some total count as centre ‘16’?
questions.explanation: There are three roll numbers with centre ID ’16’ and Some number of roll numbers with centre ID ‘17’ are there. Hence, no new roll numbers with centre ID ‘17’ is needed.

questions.question: In a certain language, ‘DRIP’ is written as ‘RKTF’ then ‘PUMP’ is written as
In a certain code, A + B means A is father of , A × B means A is mother of B, A – B mean B is sister of A, what does P × Q – R means?
options: P is mother of Q and R is sister of Q
P is father of Q and R is sister of Q
P is mother of Q and R is brother of Q
P is father of Q and Q is brother of R
questions.explanation: ⇒ P is mother of Q and R is sister of Q

questions.question: Introducing Raj, Sujata Said, “He is the son of the only daughter of the father of my father” How is Raj related to Sujata?
questions.explanation: ⇒ Raj is Cousin of Sujata.
